name: iPhone-12 capture signature
what: 30fps, Smart-HDR flat tone, cool WB, deep focus/no bokeh, floaty stabilization, AF/AE breathing — makes footage read as a real phone video, not cinema
use_when: raw UGC realism; killing the cinematic prior

name: Real-skin microtexture recipe
what: Name real skin features (fine pores, uneven tone, fine lines, under-eye puffiness, T-zone sheen) AND forbid smooth_ai_skin/waxy/poreless — never ask for 'smooth'
use_when: any human face; the #1 AI tell

name: Alive face-motion layer
what: Eye darts, gentle blinks, brow flickers, cheek/lip micro-shifts, talking mouth shapes, head bobs synced to speech — never a frozen face

name: Loose casual delivery
what: Telling-a-friend energy, small 'um', one glance away, a shrug — over-direction reads as a paid actor

name: Scored FACS/Laban beat sequence
what: Choreographed timed expression+gesture beats — polished AD feel with high production value

name: Phone-mic audio realism
what: Close slightly boomy front-mic, room echo, faint hum, breath before the line, no music — studio-clean audio betrays the fake

name: Ordinary un-staged setting
what: Normal cluttered room, flat overhead light, no styled set, no golden-hour glow

name: Reference-still identity anchor
what: For image-to-video, ONE still locks identity + skin texture; the video prompt cannot fix a waxy still; reuse the same still across all clips

name: Render negatives
what: (no on-screen text, no subtitles, no watermark) — keeps captions for the edit; product only when the beat calls for it

name: Descriptive-prose control paradigm
what: The model reads descriptive text; structured formats are packaging — best for look, skin, vibe, performance feel

name: Numeric canonical-truth paradigm
what: Explicit joint keyframes + timed contacts + effort vectors + camera keyframes — drives precise, tracked, identity-locked motion prose cannot specify; JSON alone can be sufficient
use_when: choreography, fights, dance, anime, exact motion

name: Timed contact events
what: contacts[] with region_a/region_b, start/end, type (impact|near_miss|grasp), tolerance_m — strikes land on time, hands don't phase through

name: Laban effort as numeric vectors
what: Per-interval weight/time/space/flow in [-1,1] — movement QUALITY as numbers per beat

name: Camera position keyframes
what: Explicit camera position/orientation over time — the shot path as data

name: Hard constraints + verification block
what: Machine-checkable rules in the score: identity lock >=0.95, no slow-mo, no vfx, cuts==0, contact tolerance — with failure metrics
use_when: any generation that must obey rules

name: UGC communication graph
what: hook -> problem -> product_reveal -> demonstration -> proof -> CTA with timing locks (product <3s, proof before CTA, CTA >=2.5s)

name: Beat-per-clip build
what: Models render one continuous ~4-8s shot; split the ad one clip per beat, trim, hard jump cuts — the cuts ARE authentic UGC
use_when: any ad longer than one generation

name: Char-budget discipline
what: Model input boxes cap ~2000 chars; compact combined formats carry every lever in one paste; verify with wc -c never eyeball

name: House formats: YAML-in-XML / YAML+JSON
what: Compact combined formats <2000 chars; structure keeps levers organized but the model reads the descriptive content — format is not realism

name: Timed FACS event sequence
what: Face as a SEQUENCE of AU events with start/end (brow-raise -> knit -> sharpen -> soften), intensities B-C, translated to plain language for the model

name: Duchenne smile rule
what: A genuine smile = cheeks raise WITH the mouth (AU6+AU12); mouth-only smiles read fake/creepy

name: Affect as trajectory (experienced vs displayed)
what: Valence/arousal/control as an arc over the clip, with experienced state separate from displayed performance (concealment, social smiles)

name: Laban efforts (words)
what: Weight/Time/Space/Flow qualities (light/sudden/direct/free...) + Shape (advancing/enclosing) — movement quality vocabulary, descriptive form

name: Action-atom vocabulary
what: Discrete executable units: step_in, plant, pivot, reach, strike_like, dodge, recoil, fall, recovery, grab — the shared language between extraction and generation

name: Action phase landmarks
what: Every action has onset -> anticipation apex -> acceleration -> max extension -> min target distance -> impact accent -> follow-through -> recovery -> settle; complex actions need per-limb local phases

name: Microvariation / biological noise
what: Small motion irregularity (microvariation ~0.05) prevents robotic sameness; never fully still

name: Round-trip verification
what: Re-extract the GENERATED video with the same pipeline and diff against the authored score (contact times <=50ms, trajectories, cuts) — turns eyeballing into measurement

name: Atomic deconstruction
what: Decompose video to the smallest TRANSFER-USEFUL units (one gaze shift, one foot plant, one caption pop) — each atom carries time, type, subject, value, evidence class, confidence, provenance

name: 9-level atomic ontology
what: Asset -> Sequence -> Scene -> Shot -> Beat -> Action -> Motion primitive -> Micro-event -> Frame, with 11 cross-cutting layers so one phrase never smears acting+camera+marketing together

name: Evidence classes
what: measured / detected / inferred / interpreted / authored — every claim typed by how it was obtained; Pegasus enters as interpreted/inferred, never measured

name: 4-verb transfer policy (retain/parameterize/replace/exclude)
what: Formal recreation policy: retain timing+causality+camera grammar, parameterize intensity/pace via scale factors, replace identity/voice/product/brand, exclude protected content — with per-path LOCKS carrying tolerances (e.g. first_product_visibility_s ±0.10)

name: Reverse Directorial Compilation
what: Infer a structured directorial score FROM a finished video (video -> estimated intention + control tracks); explicitly underdetermined — alternatives recorded, not resolved away

name: 8-layer fidelity
what: Judge recreation per-layer: narrative, temporal, kinematic, performative, cinematic, audiovisual, marketing, stylistic — a generation can pass one and fail another

name: Fusion precedence hierarchy
what: human lock > verified measurement > high-conf detector > fused inference > Pegasus semantics > default — TYPED not absolute (Pegasus outranks pose on narrative function; pose outranks Pegasus on wrist location)

name: Temporal snapping
what: Snap Pegasus segment times to nearest source frame / measured cut / action onset / audio transient — store BOTH raw and resolved times with method and delta

name: 8-pass Pegasus strategy
what: Pass 0 source map -> 1 scenes/shots -> 2 narrative beats -> 3 camera+edit -> 4 performance -> 5 visible actions -> 6 VFX+audio -> 7 contradiction QC. Narrow aligned passes beat one giant prompt

name: Pass 0 source map
what: First pass: broad structure, recurring entities with STABLE IDs (person_a, product_a), and candidate difficult passages — anchors all later passes

name: Pass 7 contradiction QC
what: Feed earlier-pass outputs back to Pegasus to find inconsistent entities, overlapping actions, uncertain contacts, unsupported interpretations — QC, not a substitute for programmatic validation

name: Reference-image prompting
what: Pegasus 1.5 structured prompts can include reference images — identify a specific product, keep entity references stable, check a replacement occupies the same visual role. Semantic recognition, NOT frame tracking
nl_triggers: find my product in the video

name: Clipped analysis (4s minimum)
what: Analyze difficult passages independently via start/end times; Pegasus 1.5 clipping requires a MINIMUM 4-SECOND window — pad short events and resolve times back to source clock

name: Schema-over-prompt precedence
what: Pegasus structured responses: the JSON Schema WINS over a conflicting prompt; validate client-side; check finish_reason for truncation; one analysis mode per batch

name: Named measurement stack
what: SAM 2 (promptable mask tracking), OpenPose-class (multi-person 2D pose), RAFT (dense optical flow), EMOCA (3D face + affect), audio word-times — the reproducible lane Pegasus fuses with

name: Camera/object motion separation
what: Estimate background transform after masking actors — 'dolly in' (interpretation) stays distinct from measured scale/translation curves; MotionCtrl demonstrates the same split generation-side

name: Screen-space truth (anime)
what: For stylized anime, image-space reconstruction beats forced 3D — a drawn limb legally changes length for one frame; preserve silhouette, image-space joint path, target relationship, timing, perceived force

name: Anime VFX event layer
what: Smears, impact flashes, speed lines, motion echo, energy trails, dust, held/repeated frames, background abstraction — timed events attached to actions, separable from physical motion

name: Optional physics refinement
what: DeepMimic-style physics controllers imitate reference motion with recovery — useful when terrain/proportions change; must NOT erase deliberate anime timing or screen-space exaggeration

name: 5 compilation tiers
what: Tier 1 text-only -> 2 key poses -> 3 pose-conditioned video -> 4 rigged animation intermediate (pose/mask/depth/flow control passes) -> 5 video-to-video appearance transfer. Exact movement normally needs Tier 3+
use_when: choosing the generation pipeline for a fidelity target

name: Neutral rights-safe control render
what: Tier 5 motion carrier: new-performer rig + source-derived motion + camera, NO source texture/face/voice/branding — the most direct path to preserving motion while replacing identity
nl_triggers: copy the motion not the person
use the video as motion reference

name: Provider adapter capability contract
what: Per-model JSON capability declaration (pose_video? camera_trajectory? max_duration?) -> compiler maps controls natively / via media / semantically to prompt / or reports UNSUPPORTED — never silently drops
use_when: compiling a score to any target model

name: Compiler resolution order
what: studio defaults -> production -> sequence -> scene -> shot -> beat -> event override -> human lock, with typed merge rules (scalar replace, timeline merge-by-ID, curve ops explicit) — an LLM must not improvise merges

name: Measurable marketing variables
what: time-to-first-face/-lens-address/-product/-result, product visibility DUTY CYCLE, caption density, cut count, proof-before-claim, CTA start+duration — descriptive testable variables, not conversion guarantees

name: Authenticity cue inventory
what: What makes video read creator-made: direct lens address, casual environment, self-correction, incomplete sentences, off-axis gaze, handling noise, unpolished transitions — identifiable semantically, quantifiable by measurement

name: Recreation validation metrics
what: Beat-order match, reveal error (s), visibility-curve similarity, gaze duty-cycle diff, gesture DTW distance, camera-spectrum similarity, action-onset/target-frame/reaction-delay errors, foot slip — with an acceptance-profile YAML of project thresholds

name: Failure-mode -> deterministic-response table
what: 12 named failures with fixes: truncated JSON -> reduce scope; timestamp drift -> snap; identity swap -> masks+review; false contact -> ambiguous class; UGC too polished -> enforce imperfections; motion not reproduced -> escalate tier; identity leak -> neutral render

name: Dense signals stay as media
what: Pose, masks, depth, flow, camera paths live as video/arrays/BVH/glTF — never serialized into prose or bloated text formats; text carries semantics, media carries density

name: Information-preservation accounting
what: Every pipeline hop declares what is retained, compressed, inferred, or discarded (video->Pegasus loses trajectories; video->detectors loses intention) — the discipline that makes fusion honest

name: Lab rating dimensions
what: realism / skin / motion / adherence 1-5 per run — the lab's working subset of the 8-layer fidelity model

name: profile:// modular profile system
what: Reusable versioned defaults per axis (movement/capture/camera/performance/screen_action/style) that an authoring doc inherits then overrides; precedence = local override > specific scope > profile default; hard_constraints never silently drop. Compiler-backed form of blocks
use_when: composing any score from reusable parts

name: Content/performance/style/presentation separation
what: Four separable layers: action content (walk->reach) / performance (hesitant, bound) / style transform (natural|anime|superhuman) / presentation (handheld close|locked wide). Collapsing them into one style_intensity scalar makes controlled transfer impossible
nl_triggers: change the style but keep the action

name: Typed style-transform vector
what: Style = a source_profile->target_profile transform with NAMED dimensions (timing_compression, anticipation_expansion, key_pose_hold_frames, arc_exaggeration, silhouette_separation, secondary_overlap, microvariation, graphic_smear, impact_frame, camera_emphasis) + invariants — never one style_intensity scalar

name: Protected invariants (survive stylization)
what: Declare what must NOT change under a style transform: action identity+order, participant/target identity, support/contact topology, safety class, shot purpose, critical gaze/product visibility, start/end state, locked dialogue, recovery destination. Style deviations are then explainable

name: Style ablation
what: Generate the same action changing ONE style dimension at a time (anticipation only, key-pose holds only, arc exaggeration only, smear only, full profile) to learn which controls actually produce the perceived style vs which merely add noise
nl_triggers: what actually makes it look stylized

name: Superhuman as constrained transformation
what: Superhuman motion = declared virtual-physics changes by PHASE (gravity_scale_by_phase, propulsion_impulse_scale, drag_scale, landing_response_scale) + stylized_deformation (reach/perspective extension) + presentation time-scales, with human-readable invariants (takeoff/landing contact, target readability, recovery). State WHERE energy enters and HOW it is shown separately
use_when: anime power moves, super strength/speed, gravity-defying action

name: Joint rotation representation
what: Joint tracks stored with a DECLARED rotation convention (6D rotation / rot6d avoids gimbal + interpolates cleanly; quaternions need continuity handling) and typed interpolation — precise limb orientation over time
use_when: exact joint motion, retarget, rig export

name: Kinetics: momentum, force, impulse
what: Velocity/acceleration/momentum/force/torque/impulse + inverse dynamics as estimates — encodes WEIGHT and physical consequence (a heavy strike reads different from a light one even with the same path)
use_when: physical weight, impact force, believable momentum

name: IK, retargeting, morphology
what: Inverse kinematics + retargeting + morphology/joint-limit policy — move the same motion onto a DIFFERENT body (taller, stockier, superhuman proportions) without foot-slip or self-penetration; the earliest causal layer to fix skating
use_when: identity/body swap, character proportion change, foot going through floor

name: Mannerisms, postural tone, movement signatures
what: Character identity carried by HOW they move: postural tone, one or two stable mannerisms, gesture-path directness, cultural context, recognizable movement signature — the movement-fingerprint that survives across shots
use_when: consistent character identity via motion; distinctive performers

name: Secondary / overlapping motion
what: Cloth, hair, accessories, and follow-through as STAGGERED overlapping motion driven by the primary body — a cape lags and settles after the body stops; absence reads as stiff/CG
use_when: characters with hair/cloth/capes; anime energy trails

name: Perceptual constraints (silhouette readability)
what: A THIRD constraint tier beyond hard/soft: the pose/action must READ — silhouette separation, target visible at contact, clear key poses. A physically correct motion can still be illegible

name: Motion matching / procedural execution
what: Procedural animation + motion matching + engine execution as a carrier — retrieve/blend real motion clips to satisfy the score rather than synthesizing from scratch; a Tier-3+ execution path

name: Laban effort computational proxies
what: Operationalize Laban from measurable motion: speed/accel -> Time, path directness -> Space, smoothness/jerk -> Flow, vertical drop/impact -> Weight — turns the effort WORDS into computable/checkable quantities

name: Authoring / compiler / verifier separation
what: Three logically separate roles: authoring agent PROPOSES yaml, compiler RESOLVES+validates, verifier MEASURES. Never let one model silently do all three and hide its assumptions
use_when: any agent pipeline building+checking a score

name: Correlated variation, not white noise
what: Naturalism = a LOW-FREQUENCY, task-aware, correlated variation process with limits + a seed (reproducible) — NOT independent random jitter per joint/frame, which produces vibration not life. Refines microvariation
use_when: adding human imperfection to any motion

name: Execution carrier selection
what: Pick the carrier by fidelity need: semantic->text prompt; key poses->sparse frames; exact screen-space body->pose-control video; editable contact/balance->rigged animation+IK; precise camera->camera trajectory; exact masks->segmentation video; stylized timing->edit/compositor package
use_when: choosing the generation pipeline for a fidelity target

name: Locomotion score
what: Walking/running as a multi-layer score: root trajectory + speed range, support-foot schedule / gait phases, gaze-leads-navigation, breath coupling, bounded balance corrections, motivated asymmetry — not a looped cycle

name: Two-document architecture (authoring + kinematic canonical)
what: Split a shot into an AUTHORING doc (semantic intent: 'right punch -> left parry', human/agent-authored, YAML) and a KINEMATIC CANONICAL doc (xyz joint trajectories, typed contacts, continuous-float Laban, camera path, provenance, verification metrics, JSON). Different concerns, different consumers, ONE clock. The JSON alone can drive generation (r005)
use_when: any precise-motion piece; separating intent from motion data

name: Frame-budget math
what: Precise/anime timing as explicit FRAME accounting: per-beat frame sums that validate (240 = 36+36+36+48+48+36), exposure notation (1s/2s/3s = frames-per-drawing), per-strike anticipation_f -> contact_f -> follow_through_f -> hold_f breakdowns summing exactly, held-impact minimum (>=4 frames). Timing you can verify, not vibe

name: Per-character body profile
what: Each fighter's spec: height, reach, weight, dominant hand, stance, fighting style — drives retarget/IK, contact geometry (different reach = different gap distance for the same strike), and movement signature

name: Scene spatial geometry
what: The measured space contacts/camera/root resolve against: arena dimensions, engagement range, prop separation, max aerial height. The coordinate frame that makes distances and contact tolerances meaningful
use_when: blocking, staging, aerial action, multi-actor spacing

name: Tempo / power-escalation curve
what: Dramatic pacing as an explicit curve: tempo/BPM per beat (e.g. 140 -> 200 -> 60 = build, peak, time-dilation climax = shonen rhythm) and a normalized 0->1 power-escalation curve WITH a documented tension dip. Pacing as data, not adjective
use_when: action escalation, dramatic builds, climactic finishes

name: Per-beat body mechanics
what: Full-body postural state per character per beat: torso angle, arm positions, leg drive, head tracking, shoulder state, weight-distribution %, breathing pattern — the whole-body context a strike lives inside, not just the striking limb

name: Quantified audio spec
what: Sound as measured spec synced to contact frames: frequency ranges (Hz) for rumble/screech, dB boost on impacts, doppler shifts, impact-sync tolerance (+-2 frames). Not 'add a whoosh' but a timed, quantified event track

name: Format-to-control assignment (YAML=intent, JSON=precision, XML=order/triggers)
what: Each format owns the controls it does best: YAML = creative intent, style extends:, Laban words, affect, marketing, safety, high-level constraints (human-readable+inheritable). JSON = kinematics, contacts, camera 6DoF, verification, provenance (exact machine-validated arrays). XML = ordered script, FACS/gaze/breath event triggers, VFX+audio cues via namespaces (ordered mixed content). JSONL = evidence stream; dense signals stay media. XML references the JSON by hash — one authority per quantity
use_when: deciding which format carries which control; structuring an authoring package
nl_triggers: yaml or json or xml for this

name: Format as a diversity lever
what: The SAME semantic prompt in different serializations (NL / YAML / XML / JSON / YAML+JSON / YAML+XML / JSON+XML) renders DIFFERENT model outputs. Format is NOT a quality lever (p006 — it doesn't systematically raise realism) but IS a diversity lever: swap format to generate output VARIANCE for A/B and selection. Owner explicitly values this
use_when: you want variation/options from one prompt; exploring the output space

name: 14-layer universal motion skeleton (x3 formats)
what: The canonical stack for ANY motion control: L1 intent, L2 action graph, L3 phase (global+local per-bone), L4 root/balance, L5 joint kinematics, L6 contact, L7 dynamics, L8 Laban/BESS, L9 face/affect, L10 mannerism, L11 secondary, L12 stylization/VFX, L13 presentation (camera/edit/audio), L14 verification — on a spine (identity/time/coords/authority/rights) with 3 format skeletons: YAML authors intent, JSON carries canonical numbers, XML carries ordered script+triggers. Coverage-audited against all 3 papers
use_when: building any motion score; checking nothing is dropped

name: Never default to natural language (format discipline)
what: Agents drift to NL-only prompting; here NL-only is illegal as a default. Deliver the structured format the control demands (fight/motion=JSON, intent/style=YAML, script/triggers=XML, combos for full production). NL is an OPTION delivered transparently as one labeled variant ALONGSIDE the structured forms — and emitting the same content as NL+YAML+XML+JSON is the elite A/B spread (format variance, p009)
use_when: every composed deliverable; whenever tempted to hand over one NL paragraph

name: Format mixing compiler (curate 2/3/4-format packages)
what: Curate the format combo by user intent (exact motion=JSON only; tweak-style-myself=YAML->JSON; script+SFX cues=XML+JSON; semantic-first=YAML+XML; full production=tri; options=spread+NL), then merge under the laws: one authority per quantity, XML references JSON by sha256 never restates numbers, YAML resolves down into JSON, JSON wins conflicts with a REPORT, locks survive merges, typed merges only, one shared clock
use_when: user names formats or asks for a combined package

name: Tinkering map (symptom -> field -> format)
what: When output timing/math/feel is wrong, turn the ONE owning field instead of regenerating blind: hit late=contacts[].start_f; weak punch=anticipation+impact_hold+reaction_impulse+shake+db+weight; floaty=gravity_scale+support check; robotic=correlated microvariation+local-phase lag; sliding feet=root-vs-support earliest causal layer; wrong order=temporal_relations/XML order; style not landing=one-dimension ablation; ignored numbers=escalate density or carrier. One field per iteration, log the run
use_when: user unhappy with timing, weight, pacing, order, or feel
